questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of AFSPA, 1958, what is the mandatory procedural requirement for the armed forces after arresting a person in a disturbed area?

options.text: Produce the person before a Military Court within 24 hours.
Hand over the person to the nearest police station with the least possible delay.
Detain the person for interrogation for up to 7 days before informing civil authorities.
Transfer the person to the custody of the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI).

questions.explanation: Under AFSPA, while the armed forces have the power to arrest without a warrant, they are legally required to hand over any arrested individual to the officer-in-charge of the nearest police station with the 'least possible delay', along with a report of the circumstances of the arrest.
questions.takeaway: AFSPA requires immediate handover of arrested suspects to civil police authorities to maintain a link with the regular criminal justice system.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Treatment of arrested person: Armed forces required to hand over the person to the nearest police “with least possible delay.”
